_comment: Navigation Guide — Instruction Site Template. Deploy this alongside any presentation to give users a quick orientation. 4 slides, self-contained, includes animation demo and credits.

content.headline: Welcome

nav_diagram.description: Center label: 'You are here'. Left arrow + label: '← Previous topic'. Right arrow + label: '→ Next topic'. Down arrow + label: '↓ Deeper dive (on some slides)'.

content.footer: Use arrow keys, click the controls, or swipe on mobile.

slides.speaker_notes: Orientation slide. Point out the controls in the corner.

columns.label: Horizontal Navigation
columns.description: The left and right arrows move you through the main topics. This is the primary flow — follow it in order for the full story.

columns.label: Deep Dives
columns.description: Some slides have a ↓ indicator at the bottom. That means there's optional deeper content below. Press ↓ to explore, ↑ to come back.

slides.speaker_notes: Left and right is the main flow. Down is optional depth.

content.headline: You found a deep dive! ↑ to return.
text: Deep dive slides live below the main topic.
They have more detail for curious viewers.
Press ↑ to go back up to the main flow.
Press → to skip to the next top-level topic.

slides.speaker_notes: This is what a deep dive looks like. [CLICK] first two points appear. [CLICK] navigation tips appear.

content.headline: Animations: Content That Unfolds
text: Some slides reveal content step by step.
Each click advances to the next point.
This helps the presenter control the pace.
You just saw all four — that was the demo.
content.footer_note: Each bullet appeared on a separate click — that's the animation schema in action.

slides.speaker_notes: Demo the animation. Click once per bullet. After all four: 'That's it — same pattern used throughout the presentation.'

content.headline: Built With

credits.name: OpenClaw
credits.description: Agentic framework — the engine behind this deployment

content.footer: This instruction guide was itself built from a JSON template — see presentation-starter.json

slides.speaker_notes: Quick credits. Note that this guide was built from a JSON template — the same template available to anyone building a presentation with this skill.
